tool_args.label: New York City Transit Procurements - April 2024

payload.title: NYCT Committee ACTIONS and PRESENTATIONS SUMMARY for APRIL 2024
payload.description: Board procurement item for ratification of Automated Bus Lane Enforcement contracts with Hayden AI and Seon [USA] Corp

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: Automated Bus Lane Enforcement (ABLE) systems vendor
Automated Bus Lane Enforcement (ABLE) systems vendor

warnings: entity.agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Agency names are relation context.

payload.title: MTA Deputy Chief Procurement Officer – Procurement Operations

tool_args.label: MTA Department of Diversity and Civil Rights

payload.entity_name: MTA Department of Diversity and Civil Rights

payload.description: Program for purchase, installation, operation, and maintenance of ABLE systems to enforce bus lane traffic restrictions by issuing violation notices to vehicles obstructing bus lanes
Expanded scope of ABLE program to include monitoring and ticketing vehicles obstructing bus stops as well as double-parked vehicles, following legislative approval
Three-year contract awarded to Hayden AI, Inc. for 300 ABLE systems with option for up to 200 additional systems, in estimated amount of $19,655,898

tool_args.label: Seon proposal deemed fair and reasonable by CPAU

payload.description: Seon's pricing was deemed fair and reasonable by the Cost Price Analysis Unit (CPAU)

tool_args.label: Hayden files patent infringement lawsuit against Seon

payload.description: Hayden filed the first of several lawsuits, including a preliminary injunction, against Seon and Safe Fleet (Seon's parent company) alleging patent infringement. All work with Seon regarding its ABLE system was suspended.
Modification #1 to Hayden contract awarded in estimated amount of $63,477,161 for additional ABLE systems and contract term extension
United States District Court for the Eastern District of New York issued a Memorandum & Order denying Hayden's preliminary injunction
Three-year contract awarded to Seon for up to 1,000 ABLE systems in estimated amount of $58,405,780

Limited ABLE proof-of-concept conducted with Zenco Systems, Ltd. with assistance of NYC Department of Transportation, proving the Zenco ABLE system was well suited to work with NYC enforcement laws and operating environment
Contract negotiated and awarded to Siemens for installation of 123 ABLE systems and related evidence processing; subsequently ratified by March 2019 Board
Request for Information (RFI) issued seeking additional vendors capable of furnishing ABLE systems; responses received from eight vendors

payload.claim_text: Improving the enforcement of bus lane traffic restrictions is a critical element to ensuring the success of both bus lanes and Select Bus Service (SBS)
payload.subject: Automated Bus Lane Enforcement

tool_args.label: ACE scope expansion after legislative approval

payload.claim_text: Following legislative approval to monitor and ticket vehicles obstructing bus stops as well double-parked vehicles, DOB has updated the scope of work, changed the name of this project to Automated Camera Enforcement (ACE), and requested that Procurement begin negotiations with both vendors to add the enforcement of bus stop and double-parked vehicle laws to these contracts
payload.subject: Automated Camera Enforcement

payload.claim_text: In keeping with DOB's strategy to foster competition in the enforcement space as well as reduce risk exposure by having more than one qualified vendor
payload.subject: Department of Buses

payload.claim_text: The awards to Hayden were for the purchase, installation, operation, and maintenance of up to 1,023 Automated Bus Lane Enforcement (ABLE) systems
payload.subject: Hayden AI, Inc.

payload.claim_text: The award to Seon was for the purchase, installation, operation, and maintenance of up to 1,000 ABLE systems
payload.subject: Seon [USA] Corp.

payload.claim_text: These contracts will be funded with 100 percent MTA funds. Funds for these procurements have been approved in the MTA 2020–2024 Capital Programs as well as the operating budget.
payload.subject: ABLE contracts funding
